headline: AI-assisted development broke out as the dominant technique of the month while Anthropic's pricing and safeguard decisions drew sustained criticism.
body: AI-assisted development was the clearest mover, improving with momentum 145 and breaking out alongside long-horizon planning and attribution. Anthropic dominated the new items: it walked back a policy that would have secretly limited Claude on frontier LLM tasks, drew criticism from Redis creator Antirez, and faced complaints that its Fable model burns through quotas far faster than Opus. Pricing competition sharpened as OpenAI weighed price cuts to compete with Anthropic for users. On the model side, NVIDIA shipped the Nemotron 3 series combining hybrid Mamba transformer architecture with Latent Mixture of Experts and MTP.

cited_items.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
Tell HN: Anthropic's Fable model is too expensive
OpenAI mulls slashing prices as it competes with Anthropic for users: WSJ - CNBC
Antirez on X: I believe what Anthropic is doing is *deeply* wrong
